item.title: Contact
item.description: Email: foo@xyz.com Phone: +91-123123 Mailing Address 221B, Baker Street London United Kingdom Social Facebook Twitter Google+

item.title: Experience
item.description: Experience Software Engineering Solana Labs February 2022 - Present I’ve been working full-time for Solana Labs since February as a compiler engineer. I am improving the Solang compiler and ensuring Solidity is fully compatible with the Solana blockchain. Google August 2021 - November 2021 Google has an internal programming language with limited features and aimed at security. My internship project there was coding an autocomplete system for such a language that ran in Google’s own IDE.

item.title: Misc
item.description: Other interesting stuff I’ve done Teaching While at my university robotics group, I taught two classes of machine learning and computer vision. I gave new members an introductory class of Git and wrote a guide to use the C++ STL methods in the group’s repository. Open-source contributions Openvino Notebooks: I created an example for machine translation using Intel’s OpenVino inference framework. Solang compiler: I implemented common subexpression elimination, unused variable elimination and undefined variable detection during the Hyplerledger mentorship in 2021.

item.title: Projects
item.description: Projects HTTP1.0 Server Nov. 2020 During the computer networks class at university, I created with friends a basic HTTP1.0 server that can handle html pages and files. The code is on Github. LangInterpreter Jun. 2020 During a compilers course at my undergraduate studies, I developed with a friend an interpreter for a programming laguage with Portuguese constructs. The code is available on Gitub. iOS/Android Monitor Jun. 2020 During the Operating Systems course, I developed in a group project a system monitor for Android and another one for iOS.

item.title: Reads
item.description: Reading Reading is one of my favourite activities. I usually keep up to date with the world by reading Wired UK and The Economist. Regarding books, I have a preference for those who explain the world around me and the latest developments in history. Books Each of these books has had an impact in how I understand what happens in society and in the technology world.

item.title: Research/Publications
item.description: Research/Publications L. Steuernagel, M. R. O. A. Maximo. Trajectory prediction for SSL robots using seq2seq neural networks. Accepted at the 25th RoboCup International Symposium 2022, but pending publication. L. Steuernagel, M. R. O. A. Maximo, L. A. Pereira and C. A. A. Sanches, “Convolutional Neural Network with Inception-like Module for Ball and Goalpost Detection in a Small Humanoid Soccer Robot,” 2020 Latin American Robotics Symposium (LARS), 2020 Brazilian Symposium on Robotics (SBR) and 2020 Workshop on Robotics in Education (WRE), 2020, pp.
